title: ferray-polynomial: f64-only — no generic float or complex support
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

All polynomial types use f64 coefficients. There's no way to have f32 polynomials or complex-coefficient polynomials. NumPy's polynomial classes work with any numeric type. While f64 is fine for most use cases, complex coefficients are needed for certain signal processing applications, and f32 would reduce memory for large polynomial arrays. The Poly trait and all structs should be generic over a coefficient type T: Float (and eventually T: ComplexFloat for complex).
